text: The rawrr package provides access to proprietary Thermo Fisher Scientific Orbitrap instrument data as a stand-alone R package or serves as a backend for the Bioconductor Spectra package.
source_excerpt: The package provides access to proprietary Thermo Fisher Scientific Orbitrap instrument data as a stand-alone R package or serves as [MsRawFileReaderBackend](https://github.com/cpanse/MsBackendRawFileReader) for the Bioconductor [Spectra](https://bioconductor.org/packages/Spectra/) package.

text: The example file 20181113_010_autoQC01.raw contains Fourier-transformed Orbitrap spectra recorded on a Thermo Fisher Scientific Q Exactive HF mass spectrometer in positive mode.
source_excerpt: The example file `20181113_010_autoQC01.raw` used throughout this manuscript contains Fourier-transformed Orbitrap spectra (FTMS) recorded on a Thermo Fisher Scientific Q Exactive HF in positive mode (+).

text: The analyzed sample consisted of the iRT peptide mix in a tryptic BSA digest separated using a 20 min linear gradient on C18 reversed-phase material.
source_excerpt: The analyzed sample consisted of the iRT peptide mix (Biognosys) in a tryptic BSA digest (NEB) and was separated applying a 20 min linear gradient on C18 reversed-phase material

text: The readSpectrum function returns a rawrrSpectrum object or rawrrSpectrumSet.
source_excerpt: Individual scans or scan collections (sets) can be read by the function `readSpectrum()` which returns a `rawrrSpectrum` object or `rawrrSpectrumSet`.

text: Linear regression can convert observed peptide retention times into iRT values and vice versa for retention time calibration.
source_excerpt: By applying linear regression, one can convert observed peptide retention times (RTs) into dimensionless scores termed iRT values and *vice versa* [@Escher2012]. This can be used for retention time calibration/prediction.

text: Fitted iRT regression models provide information about LC-MS run performance.
source_excerpt: fitted iRT regression models provide highly valuable information about LC-MS run performance.

text: The iRT peptides were separated on a 20 minute linear gradient from 5% buffer B to 35% buffer B using C18 reversed-phase material.
source_excerpt: the iRT peptides were separated on a 20 min linear gradient from 5% buffer B to 35% buffer B using C18 reversed-phase material

text: The iRT peptide GAGSSEPVTGLDAK is defined to have a zero score on the iRT scale.
source_excerpt: The intercept (a) is equal to the predicted RT of iRT peptide `GAGSSEPVTGLDAK` since it was defined to have a zero score on the iRT scale.

text: The iRT regression R-squared indicates that RTs behave highly linearly for the example data.
source_excerpt: The corresponding R-squared indicates that the RTs behave highly linear.
